label: GitLab CE
description: Self-hosted Git repository manager with CI/CD, issue tracking and container registry. Deploy a local GitLab to use as a GitOps repository source with Flux.

form.name: releaseName
form.label: Release Name
form.help: Helm release name — used as a prefix for all Kubernetes resources.

form.name: namespace
form.label: Namespace
form.help: Kubernetes namespace to deploy GitLab into. Created automatically if it does not exist.

form.name: gitlab.global.hosts.domain
form.label: Ingress Domain
form.help: Base domain for GitLab ingress. GitLab will be reachable at gitlab.<domain>. All sub-services (registry, pages) derive their hostnames from this value.

form.name: gitlab.global.hosts.https
form.label: Enable HTTPS
form.help: Serve GitLab over HTTPS. MUST be true when a Keycloak security profile is selected in Step 1 — OAuth callbacks reject http:// redirect URIs and the OmniAuth provider YAML built by the view will mirror this setting. Disable only for HTTP-only dev clusters without SSO.

form.name: gitlab.global.ingress.class
form.label: Ingress Class
form.help: IngressClass to use for GitLab web and SSH ingress (e.g. nginx, traefik).

form.name: gitlabTlsClusterIssuer
form.label: Ingress TLS ClusterIssuer (cert-manager)
form.help: Pick a cert-manager ClusterIssuer. When set, the chart's Ingresses get cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer + tls.enabled, so cert-manager auto-issues a Certificate and populates spec.tls[]. Leave blank only if you will provide a TLS Secret out-of-band (otherwise HTTPS will be unreachable and OIDC SSO will 302-loop back to the sign-in page).

form.name: gitlab.global.ingress.configureCertmanager
form.label: Configure cert-manager (chart's own Issuer)
form.help: Let the GitLab chart create its OWN Let's Encrypt Issuer. Keep false if you picked a ClusterIssuer above — that's the right path for internal clusters.

form.name: gitlab.certmanager.install
form.label: Install bundled cert-manager
form.help: GitLab can install its own cert-manager. Keep false if the cluster already has one.

form.name: gitlab.nginx-ingress.enabled
form.label: Install bundled nginx-ingress
form.help: GitLab bundles an nginx ingress controller. Keep false if the cluster already has an ingress controller.

form.name: gitlab.prometheus.install
form.label: Install bundled Prometheus
form.help: GitLab bundles its own Prometheus instance. Disable if you already have cluster-wide monitoring (e.g. kube-prometheus-stack).

form.name: gitlab.gitlab-runner.install
form.label: Install GitLab Runner
form.help: Deploy a GitLab Runner alongside GitLab. Can be enabled later via upgrade.

form.name: gitlab.registry.enabled
form.label: Enable Container Registry
form.help: Enable the built-in container registry (registry.<domain>). Requires additional ingress and storage. Disable for minimal dev installs.

form.name: gitlab.global.edition
form.label: Edition
form.help: CE is free and suitable for dev/GitOps use. EE requires a valid licence key.

form.name: gitlab.gitlab.toolbox.backups.cron.enabled
form.label: Enable scheduled backups
form.help: Enable the GitLab toolbox backup cron job. Keep false for dev installs.

form.name: gitlab.global.appConfig.initialDefaults.signupEnabled
form.label: Allow public sign-up
form.help: Allow users to self-register. Disable for private dev instances.

form.name: gitlabRootPasswordSecret
form.label: Initial root password Secret (optional)
form.help: Kubernetes Secret in the install namespace whose 'password' key holds the password for the built-in 'root' user. Leave blank to let the chart auto-generate one (stored in Secret '<releaseName>-gitlab-initial-root-password', key 'password' — fetchable via 'kubectl get secret ... -o jsonpath=...'). Use this field when you want a known/managed password (e.g. provisioned via Vault) so 'root' can promote SSO users via Admin Area > Users.

fields.name: gitlab.global.appConfig.omniauth.syncProfileFromProvider[0]
fields.label: Sync profile from SSO
fields.help: Provider name to sync name/email from. Leave as 'openid_connect' for Keycloak SSO.

fields.name: gitlab.global.appConfig.omniauth.syncProfileAttributes[0]
fields.label: Sync profile attributes
fields.help: Attributes to sync from the OIDC provider (e.g. email).

bindings.name: ingress-tls-defaults
bindings._description: Make spec.tls[] actually land on the chart's Ingresses (the chart only adds it when tls.enabled is explicitly true) and force HTTP→HTTPS at the edge so the OmniAuth POST does not lose its Secure session cookie and 302-loop back to /users/sign_in.

bindings.name: ingress-tls-cluster-issuer
bindings._description: Wire the user-picked cert-manager ClusterIssuer via annotation. cert-manager's ingress-shim watches Ingresses with this annotation + tls[] and auto-creates the Certificate + Secret. Skipped silently if the user left the field blank.

bindings.name: initial-root-password
bindings._description: Point the chart at a user-provided Secret holding the 'root' password (key 'password'). Skipped when blank — the chart then auto-generates and stores it in Secret '<releaseName>-gitlab-initial-root-password'.

bindings.name: trusted-ca-customcas
bindings._description: Mount the merged Ambari/company truststore (Secret '<releaseName>-truststore' — created automatically by the security-profile step earlier in the deploy plan) into every GitLab pod via the chart's customCAs volume. The same Secret also carries the JKS for JVM services; we MUST pin keys=['ca.crt'] to avoid feeding the binary JKS to update-ca-certificates as if it were a PEM bundle. See docs/OUTBOUND_TLS_TRUSTSTORE.md for the Secret schema.
